name: CAU Exposed: Sexual Violence, Financial Crimes & Institutional Failure
description: Documenting 9 confirmed sexual violence perpetrators across 7 departments, Doosan financial crimes, and systematic cover-ups at Chung-Ang University (CAU), Seoul, Korea. 1998–2026.

mainEntity.name: Chung-Ang University (CAU) Sexual Violence & Governance Failure Timeline
mainEntity.description: Chronological documentation of confirmed sexual violence cases, financial crimes, and institutional failures at Chung-Ang University from 1998 to 2026

itemListElement.name: Dance Dept Professor: Habitual Sexual Assault of Male Students — Convicted
itemListElement.description: Three male students filed criminal charges against their CAU Dance Department professor for habitual sexual assault. The professor was also Director of the Korea National Dance Company. Convicted to 3 years (1999), 1 year on appeal (2001), re-indicted for witness tampering (2003).

itemListElement.name: Doosan Group Takeover — Democratic Governance Abolished
itemListElement.description: Doosan Group acquired the CAU foundation. Within 80 days, Park Yong-sung abolished the direct presidential election system, replacing it with a Doosan-controlled appointment. CAU ceased to function as a self-governing academic institution.

itemListElement.name: Woori Bank 10-Billion-Won Kickback Scheme
itemListElement.description: Former CAU president Park Beom-hoon allegedly exchanged exclusive campus banking rights for a 10 billion won (~$9M USD) 'donation'. Prosecutors found part of the money was never used for the school.

itemListElement.name: CAU President Park Beom-hoon Publicly Objectifies Female Students
itemListElement.description: CAU president Park Beom-hoon made a public statement rendering female students as objects for male sexual appetite. Reported in Ohmynews (Feb 23, 2009) and Kyunghyang Shinmun (Feb 26, 2009).

itemListElement.name: Student Press Suppressed; Activists Surveilled by Doosan Administration
itemListElement.description: CAU administration suppressed a critical student journal and the student union sued the Doosan foundation over alleged surveillance of student activists — dismantling the advocacy infrastructure victim-survivors depend on.

itemListElement.name: Professor K — Serial Sexual Assault of Graduate Students + Research Fund Embezzlement
itemListElement.description: Professor K (Asian Cultural Studies) habitually sexually assaulted female graduate students for 6+ years. Four victim-survivors filed simultaneous reports in May 2018. The CAU Human Rights Center recommended full dismissal (파면) and found concurrent research grant embezzlement.

location.name: Chung-Ang University, Department of Asian Cultural Studies, Seoul

itemListElement.keywords: sexual assault, embezzlement, CAU, Asian Cultural Studies

itemListElement.name: Lecturer A, Sculpture — Sexual Assault of 4 Female Students, Motel Abduction Attempt, 5-Year Institutional Cover-Up
itemListElement.description: On the night of June 12–13, 2013, adjunct lecturer A assaulted four female students and attempted to take them to a motel. Colleague Prof B pressured victims to settle privately. CAU cited lecturer's part-time status to avoid formal disciplinary record. Suppressed for 5 years until alumni association publicly exposed it in March 2018.

location.name: Chung-Ang University, Department of Sculpture, Seoul

itemListElement.keywords: sexual assault, cover-up, adjunct loophole, CAU, Sculpture

itemListElement.name: Professor J — Admitted Sexual Assault; HR Committee Delayed Proceedings to Allow Resignation; Rehired at Russian University
itemListElement.description: Professor J admitted sexual assault. The HR committee deliberately delayed proceedings allowing J to resign rather than be dismissed, avoiding a disciplinary record. J was subsequently rehired at Russia's North-Eastern Federal University.

itemListElement.name: CAU Foundation Accounting Fraud Investigation — 20.3 Billion Won
itemListElement.description: CAU's educational foundation was placed under investigation for accounting fraud totaling 20.3 billion won (~$18M USD). Foundation Chairman Park Yong-sung resigned simultaneously from CAU, Doosan Heavy Industries, and the Korean Olympic Committee.

itemListElement.name: Park Beom-hoon Arrested — Bribery, Corruption, Nepotism — Convicted and Imprisoned
itemListElement.description: Former CAU president and Blue House Chief Secretary for Education Park Beom-hoon was arrested after 19 hours of questioning. Charges included receiving 1.8B won via front foundation, real estate kickbacks, pressuring Ministry of Education to approve campus merger saving Doosan billions, and daughter's nepotistic faculty appointment. Convicted and imprisoned; released May 2017.

itemListElement.name: Foundation Chairman Threatens Named Female Professor by Gender
itemListElement.description: Foundation Chairman Park Yong-sung emailed approximately 20 opposing faculty members threatening to 'axe those begging to be axed' and specifically named a female professor in the German Language Department as a dismissal target — documented gender-targeted institutional intimidation.

itemListElement.name: Professor L — Same Department as J and M — Dismissed for Sexual Assault of Female Graduate Student
itemListElement.description: Professor L in the same unnamed department as the 2014 (Prof J) and 2018 (Prof M) cases sexually assaulted a female graduate student and was dismissed (해임). Three different perpetrators in one department across four years is a documented departmental culture.

itemListElement.name: Professor B, Cultural Studies — Multiple Sexual Violence Reports; Full Dismissal (파면) Recommended
itemListElement.description: Multiple victim-survivors reported Professor B of CAU's Cultural Studies department for repeated sexual violence. The Human Rights Center recommended full dismissal (파면) — the harshest sanction — in May 2018.

location.name: Chung-Ang University, Department of Cultural Studies, Seoul

itemListElement.name: Japanese Literature Professor — Habitual Sexual Assault at Drinking Gatherings
itemListElement.description: A professor in CAU's Japanese Literature department was reported in national press during the 2018 MeToo wave for habitually sexually assaulting students at drinking gatherings (술자리).

location.name: Chung-Ang University, Department of Japanese Literature, Seoul

itemListElement.keywords: sexual assault, drinking gathering, CAU, Japanese Literature

itemListElement.name: Professor M — Drug-Facilitated Rape; Denied All; Dismissed but Reportedly Retained in Researcher Database
itemListElement.description: Professor M in the same cluster department as J and L committed drug-facilitated rape. Denied all allegations. Dismissed (해임, 3-year bar). NamuWiki reports M remained listed in the Korean Researcher Information database as a CAU employee after dismissal — suggesting planned rehiring.
itemListElement.keywords: drug-facilitated rape, dismissed, CAU, researcher database

itemListElement.name: Professor A, English Literature — Drug-Facilitated Sexual Assault; Victim Diagnosed with PTSD; Perpetrator Re-Contacted Victim
itemListElement.description: Professor A sexually violated an undergraduate student incapacitated by prescribed sleeping medication and alcohol. After the assault, A denied all allegations, continued calling the victim late at night, and requested meetings. Victim was diagnosed with PTSD. Emergency Committee documented A as having approached at least two other female students on the same night.

location.name: Chung-Ang University, Department of English Literature, Seoul

itemListElement.keywords: drug-facilitated sexual assault, PTSD, English Literature, CAU

itemListElement.name: Professor A Dismissed (해임) — Students Protested Demanding Harsher 파면
itemListElement.description: CAU confirmed the final dismissal (해임) of English Literature Professor A on June 11, 2019. Students staged protests at the campus main entrance demanding the permanent ban of 파면 rather than the 3-year bar of 해임. Across all 9 confirmed CAU sexual violence perpetrators (1998–2019): zero confirmed permanent bans (파면).

itemListElement.name: Partner Pages Blank: Ajou & Hongik Universities — March 2026
itemListElement.description: Gender Watchdog captured screenshots on March 8, 2026 showing that the official partner university pages of Ajou University and Hongik University listed no active partnership with Chung-Ang University. Pattern mirrors Dongguk University false partnership documentation.
itemListElement.keywords: partnership, false claim, Ajou, Hongik, CAU
